message.content:
## Web Searcher: practitioner/agent field perspective

Research question: "National Life Group (NLG) / Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW) TERM LIFE INSURANCE products — comprehensive product reference. Cover: (1) the full current term lineup and product names (e.g. the "G" guaranteed level-premium series Term 10-G / 15-G / 20-G / 30-G, form series ICC18-20522, and any other current term products like LSW Level Term / annually-renewable term); (2) issue ages and available level term lengths; (3) the level-premium period then annually-renewable-to-age-95 structure, whether the policy has cash value/dividends (it does not); (4) the conversion privilege — window (first 20 years or to age 70 if earlier), no evidence of insurability, equivalent rate class, what permanent products it converts to; (5) the Accelerated Benefit Rider (ABR) living-benefit suite available on term (Terminal, Chronic, Critical Illness, Critical Injury, Alzheimer's) — cost (no additional premium), triggers, discounted/condition-specific payout, and per-insured lifetime benefit limits and how they vary by state; (6) premium structure and rate classes (Elite/Preferred/Select/Standard/Express, tobacco/non-tobacco); (7) state availability variations; (8) foreign-national / non-US-resident rules for term (minimum face, US nexus/solicitation/delivery requirements, whether Brazil/"B" country is eligible, whether term is even offered to foreign nationals). Prioritize NLG/LSW primary sources: nationallife.com, the Life Product Quick Reference Chart, agent guides, product guides, rider brochures, and the Foreign National Guidelines. Distinguish guaranteed/structural facts from rate-sheet-driven current rates (which change often). Produce a cited report with source URLs."

Your angle: **practitioner/agent field perspective** — Independent agent/brokerage commentary and IMO field guides surface practical realities — no cash value/dividends, Express vs full underwriting tradeoffs, competitiveness — that fill gaps or corroborate the primary-source structural claims.
Search query: `LSW National Life term life insurance agent review rates illustration Elite Express underwriting cash value dividends`

## Task
Use WebSearch with the query above (or a refined version). Return the top 4-6 most relevant results.
Rank by relevance to the ORIGINAL question, not just the search query. Skip obvious SEO spam/content farms.
Include a short snippet capturing why each result is relevant.

Structured output only.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files) or Word templates (.dotx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', '.dotx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx or .dotx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:morning: Render the user's morning brief as a styled HTML artifact, or set it up as a recurring weekday task. Use when: morning brief, daily brief, set up my morning brief, what's my day look like, run my brief.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx or .potx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx or .potx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates (.potx), layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx or .potx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx or .potx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .xltx, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Links: [{"title":"Is Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW) a Good Company To Buy From? | The Annuity Expert","url":"https://www.annuityexpertadvice.com/reviews/lsw/"},{"title":"National Life Group / LSW Annuity Review (2026) | My Annuity Store, Inc.","url":"https://myannuitystore.com/annuity-insurance-companies/national-life-group/"},{"title":"Life Insurance Co of the Southwest (LSW) - Profile and Review | AnnuityAdvantage","url":"https://www.annuityadvantage.com/insurance-companies/life-insurance-company-of-the-southwest-lsw/"},{"title":"Trusted Quote | Life Insurance Company of the Southwest","url":"https://trustedquote.com//life-insurance-company-of-the-southwest/"},{"title":"National Life Group Life Insurance Review 2026: Pros & Cons - NerdWallet","url":"https://www.nerdwallet.com/insurance/life/national-life-group-life-insurance-review"},{"title":"Life Insurance Underwriting Guide","url":"https://www.totalfinancial.com/carriers/downloads/lsw/lsw-uw1.pdf"},{"title":"Life Insurance Co. of The Southwest Review & Ratings","url":"https://www.bestlifequote.com/blog/life-insurance-company-of-the-southwest-review/"},{"title":"National Life Group Insurance Reviews 2026","url":"https://agencyheight.com/insurance-reviews/national-life-group-insurance/"},{"title":"Life Insurance Company of the Southwest Review & Ratings (2025) | USInsuranceAgents.com","url":"https://www.usinsuranceagents.com/reviews/life-insurance-company-of-the-southwest/"},{"title":"Life Insurance Company of the Southwest Review","url":"https://annuityeducator.com/reviews/life-insurance-company-of-the-southwest"}]

I've completed a web search for information about LSW National Life term life insurance. Here's what I found:

## Company Overview & Financial Strength

LSW has a Comdex score of 90, placing it among the top-tier insurers in terms of overall financial strength. Both entities carry an A+ AM Best rating, an A+ from S&P, an A1 from Moody's, and a Comdex composite score of 90 – firmly in the top tier of financial strength.

## Products & Coverage Options

LSW offers term, universal life, IUL, fixed annuities, and indexed annuities. National Life Group offers a variety of life insurance policies, including some that can earn dividends based on the company's financial performance. Many of its policies have built-in living benefits, such as riders that can pay out a portion of your policy if you're diagnosed with Alzheimer's or another chronic or critical illness.

## Rates & Illustration Tools

Many customers praise LSW for offering competitive rates on their life insurance policies. NLG consistently ranks near the top of national rate tables while carrying A+ ratings from three agencies.

## Underwriting & Agent Access

Several customers appreciate the easy and straightforward application process for LSW's life insurance products. You'll need to connect with an agent to get a quote or buy a policy. You'll have to speak directly with a National Life Group agent to learn more or apply for a policy.

## Cash Value & Dividends

Since National Life Group is a mutual company — one of the nation's first mutual companies, in fact — whole life policy owners can receive dividends which can subsidize the cost of this coverage.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"Accelerated Benefits Riders (ABR) Agent Guide Products issued by","url":"https://theinsgroup.net/images/stories/LSW/Life/LSW_Living_Benefits_Rider.pdf"},{"title":"LSW Term Portfolio/Rates Product Guide Term","url":"http://theinsgroup.net/images/stories/LSW/Life/LSW_Level_Term_Product_Guide.pdf"},{"title":"Life Insurance Underwriting Guide","url":"https://www.totalfinancial.com/carriers/downloads/lsw/lsw-uw1.pdf"},{"title":"National Life Insurance Company","url":"https://merrillconnect.iscorp.com/nlg/viewDocument.action?itemNbr=103886"},{"title":"Products issued by National Life Insurance Company","url":"https://lbfg.net/wp-content/uploads/National-Life-LSW-UW-Guide.pdf"},{"title":"Products issued by Life Insurance Company of the Southwest ®","url":"https://theinsgroup.net/images/stories/LSW/Life/LSW_SecurePlus_Product_Guide.pdf"},{"title":"Product issued by","url":"http://theinsgroup.net/images/stories/LSW/Life/LSW_FlexLife_Product_Guide.pdf"},{"title":"Product","url":"http://www.stephens-matthews.com/i/lsw/2019LifeProductQuickReference.pdf"},{"title":"LSW Level Term 20-G Term Life Insurance Prepared on September 6, 2016 for","url":"https://www.labonteinsurance.net/uploads/8/7/5/5/87554500/lsw_20_year_term_m45__500k.pdf"},{"title":"Producer Commission Schedule","url":"https://www.empowerbrokerage.com/downloads/Carriers/NLG/GAF_8175-0116.pdf"}]

The search has successfully found relevant LSW National Life product guides and information about their term life insurance products. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## LSW Level Term Products

LSW Level Term 10, LSW Level Term 15, LSW Level Term 20, and LSW Level Term 30 (both G and NG plans) are low-cost, convertible term life insurance policies with level premium periods and level death benefits.

## Conversion and Accelerated Benefits Rider Features

LSW Level Term 20-G policies are convertible during the first 15 years from the date of issue or until age 70 if earlier, but in no case less than 5 years from date of issue, without evidence of insurability to any single life permanent plan of life insurance.

## Accelerated Benefits Riders

The Accelerated Benefits Riders 1, 2 and 3 all allow accelerated payment of up to 100% of the policy or rider death benefit. Accelerated Benefits Rider 1 (ABR 1) is available in case of terminal illness; Accelerated Benefits Rider 2 (ABR 2) is available in case of chronic illness. Accelerated Benefits Rider 3 (ABR3) is available in case of critical illness.

## No Cash Value

LSW Level Term products are term insurance policies, which means they do not build cash value. Accelerated Benefit Riders are optional, available with no additional premium, and may not be available in all states.

The documents found include the LSW Level Term Product Guide and the Accelerated Benefits Riders Agent Guide, which are the official product materials for these offerings.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: I have strong, relevant results. Compiling the top findings prioritized against the original comprehensive-reference question.

results.title: LSW Term Portfolio / Rates Product Guide (Level Term) — agent product guide
results.snippet: Primary agent-facing product guide covering the LSW Level Term lineup (Term 10/15/20/30, both G guaranteed and NG non-guaranteed variants). Confirms level-premium periods, level death benefit, convertibility, and that these are term policies building no cash value. Directly corroborates the structural claims in items (1), (2), (3), and (4) of the research question from a field/IMO-distributed source.

results.title: Accelerated Benefits Riders (ABR) Agent Guide — LSW / National Life
results.snippet: The living-benefits (ABR) agent guide detailing ABR1 (terminal illness), ABR2 (chronic illness), and ABR3 (critical illness), each allowing accelerated payment of up to 100% of the death benefit, offered at no additional premium, with a note that availability varies by state. Core source for item (5) on the ABR suite, triggers, cost, and state variation.

results.title: LSW Level Term 20-G sample illustration (M45, $500k)
results.snippet: An actual agent-generated LSW Level Term 20-G illustration showing the concrete conversion privilege language: convertible during the first 15 years or to age 70 if earlier (but not less than 5 years from issue), without evidence of insurability, to any single-life permanent plan. Grounds the conversion-window mechanics in item (4) with a real field document rather than marketing summary.

results.title: LSW / National Life Life Product Quick Reference chart
results.snippet: The Life Product Quick Reference Chart explicitly named in the research prompt — a one-page agent reference listing the full life lineup including term products, issue ages, and the permanent plans term converts into. Directly supports items (1), (2), and the conversion-target question in (4).

results.title: National Life / LSW Life Insurance Underwriting Guide
results.snippet: Underwriting guide covering rate/risk classes and requirements, relevant to item (6) rate classes (Elite/Preferred/Select/Standard, tobacco/non-tobacco) and the Express vs. full-underwriting tradeoff highlighted in the agent-perspective angle. Also bears on issue ages and substandard handling.

results.title: Is LSW a Good Company To Buy From? — The Annuity Expert (practitioner review)
results.snippet: Independent practitioner review corroborating financial strength (A+ AM Best, Comdex ~90) and the mutual-company/dividend context that distinguishes NLG's participating permanent products from its non-participating term (which pays no dividends and builds no cash value). Useful field-perspective corroboration for competitiveness and for clarifying the cash-value/dividend distinction in item (3).
